titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Barbara Henning Papers
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday August 27, 2021 at 03:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 17 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 18 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Writings, 1980-2016. II. Correspondence, 1970-2015. III. Personal Papers, 1974-2017. IV. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 2007.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Barbara Henning Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of writings, correspondence, personal papers, audio and video recordings, digital files, and other papers by and relating to American poet and writer Barbara Henning. Materials including notes, drafts, and manuscripts document Henning's career as a poet, writer, and editor, and provide insight into her creative process. Correspondents include Charles Alexander, Charles Baxter, E. M. Broner, Bill Kushner, Harry Mathews, Harryette Mullen, and Maureen Owen.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Barbara Henning Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: Barbara Henning (1948-)
bioghist.p: Barbara Henning, American poet and writer, is the author of eighteen books of poetry and fiction, as well as a series of twenty artists' books combining poetry with photography. She was born on October 26, 1948 in Detroit, Michigan. She received both a B.A. and M.A. in English from Wayne State University in 1981 and 1983, respectively, and later earned her Ph.D. in English from The Graduate School of Union Institute in 1988. In 1983, she moved to New York City, residing in the East Village for many years.

Henning has published eight poetry collections, beginning with Smoking in the Twilight Bar (1988); other titles include Love Makes Thinking Dark (1995) and Cities and Memory (2010). Her five novels include Thirty Miles to Rosebud (2009) and Just Like That (2018). She edited Looking Up Harryette Mullen (2011), a book of correspondence and interviews with African American poet Harryette Mullen, and collected the short stories of Beat writer Bobbie Louise Hawkins in The Selected Prose of Bobbie Louise Hawkins (2012). Henning's poetry and prose have been published in journals such as Dispatches , Talisman , The Brooklyn Rail , and Journal of Poetics Research . Henning is Professor Emerita at Long Island University Brooklyn, where she taught from 1988 to 2005. She has also taught at Naropa University in Colorado and the University of Arizona.

Henning was previously married to Allen Saperstein, with whom she had two children, Micah and Linnée. She currently lives in New York City and has also lived in Detroit, Santa Fe, New Mexico, and Tucson, Arizona, as well as Mysore, India, where she studied yoga for a year.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings consist of journals, notes, drafts, page proofs, printed works, research materials, reviews, advertising material, correspondence, and related materials pertaining to Henning's published and unpublished poetry collections, novels, editing projects, artists' books, essays, interviews, and other works.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Journals, Books, Artists' Books, and Shorter Works.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains approximately 75 handwritten journals from 1990 to 2015 which formed the groundwork of Henning's writings. Many journals also include printouts of digital files, loose drafts, correspondence, clippings, or other notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of files for Henning's fourteen published and unpublished books, including seven poetry collections, four novels, and two works edited by Henning: Looking Up Harryette Mullen (2011) and The Selected Prose of Bobbie Louise Hawkins (2012). It also contains files for a proposed "Gender and Writing Project" anthology in collaboration with Bill Kushner, since abandoned, that features submissions of other poets' writings. Materials include handwritten, typescript, and computer printout drafts, many with corrections, as well as page proofs, illustrations, advertising material, and reviews, dating approximately 1983 to 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

did.unittitle: Early work on individual sequences, typescript printouts, corrected
Drafts of poems, typescript printouts, some corrected
Early drafts, typescript printouts, corrected, with correspondence
Draft for Chax Press, typescript printouts, corrected
Drafts, typescript printouts, corrected, with correspondence
Page proofs, corrected, with correspondence and advertisements
Detective Sentences , drafts, typescript printouts, corrected, with notes, correspondence, and visuals
Correspondence with Bill Kushner and writers
Original journals used to construct first draft, typescript printouts, corrected
Drafts, typescript printouts, corrected, with notes
"An Interview with Harryette Mullen," printed version in The Poetry Project Newsletter , with correspondence and postcards
Drafts, typescript printouts, some corrected, with correspondence
Drafts, typescript printouts, some corrected, with proofreaders' comments by Martine Bellen
Draft, typescript printouts, corrected, with graphics
Love Makes Thinking Dark , book cover and graphics
Drafts, typescript printouts, corrected, with notes

title.part: The Selected Prose of Bobbie Louise Hawkins

did.unittitle: Drafts, typescript printouts, some corrected, with notes and correspondence
Drafts of poems, holograph and typescript printouts, corrected
Drafts, typescript printouts, corrected, with notes and research
"Hegira" drafts, typescript printouts, corrected, with notes and research
Drafts, typescript printouts, corrected, with correspondence, holograph notes, and research
Draft fragments, typescript printouts, corrected, with correspondence, notes, and research

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains twenty-two limited edition "photo-poem pamphlets," blending poetry with personal photography, which were created and self-published by Henning from 1994 to 2014. Included are several early versions of works that would later be published in Henning's full-length books.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: Collected artists' books, and Drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The final printed versions of Henning's artists' books, previously housed as a set in a single binder. Many files also contain prints of the photographs used in these works.

title.part: How to Read and Write in the Dark

did.unittitle: Found in the Park , with photographs
Aham Asmi Aham Asmi , with photographs
The Animal I Am , with photographs
Thirty Miles to Rosebud , with photographs
An Arc Falling into the Bougainvillea , with photographs
Twirling, the Spirit Flies Off Like a Falcon , with photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials generated during the creative process, including typescript drafts, holograph notes, page proofs, and graphics.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

did.unittitle: Aerial View , page proofs, corrected, with drafts, typescript printouts, and holograph notes
The Animal I Am , page proofs, corrected, with drafts, typescript printouts
An Arc Falling into the Bougainvillea , page proofs, corrected, with drafts, typescript printouts, corrected
Black Grapes , page proofs, corrected, with drafts, typescript printouts, corrected
Cities and Memory , page proofs, corrected, with drafts, and research
The Dinner , drafts, typescript printouts, corrected, with holograph notes
Found in the Park , page proofs and draft, typescript printouts, corrected
Hari Om , page proofs, corrected, with drafts, typescript printouts, some corrected, notes, and graphics

title.part: How to Read and Write in the Dark

did.unittitle: Little Tesuque , page proofs, corrected, with drafts, typescript printouts, and research
My Animal Eyeball , drafts, typescript printouts, corrected
Teacher Training , page proofs of "Now I Shall be a Good Truant," corrected
Tha Kita Thaka , paste-ups and photocopy, with draft, typescript printouts, corrected
Thirty Miles to Rosebud , page proofs, corrected, with drafts, typescript printouts, corrected
Twelve Green Rooms , drafts, typescript printouts, corrected, with holograph notes and research
Twirling, the Spirit Flies Off Like a Falcon , page proofs, corrected, with drafts, typescript printouts, corrected, holograph notes, and research

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of Henning's essays, interviews of other writers, talks, notes, and assorted stand-alone poems, essay-poems and prose poems, as well as Henning's reviews of other writers' work. There is also a comprehensive collection of photocopy tear sheets containing many of Henning's published writings, including work published in journals and magazines.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: Tear sheets, and Individual works. Arranged in chronological order.

did.unittitle: "The World Was Stone Cold: Basic Writing in an Urban University," essay, printed version in College English , drafts, and correspondence
"Falling in Love with Your Memories," short memoir, printed version, drafts, and notes
Review of Solution Simulacra by Gloria Frym for The Poetry Project Newsletter , drafts
"The Content of History will be Poetry," essay-poem on Charles Olson's The Special View of History , drafts, research, and correspondence
"The Content of History will be Poetry," essay-poem on Charles Olson's The Special View of History , drafts, research, and correspondence
"A Telephone Interview with Maureen Owen on Erosion's Pull ," drafts, notes, and correspondence
"Invisible City," poems written for event in Tucson
"The Dark Labyrinth of Conceptual Poetries," response to Conceptual Poetry Conference in Tucson, with draft, notes, and program
Review of Absolutely Eden by Bobbie Louise Hawkins, drafts and correspondence
"Between the Mountains: My Poetry Group in Tucson (2006-2010) & An Interview with Charles Alexander and Tenney Nathanson," drafts and correspondence
"An Interview with Harry Mathews," drafts, research, and correspondence
Notes and prints for book party discussion at Poets House, 4/27/2011
"Interview: Barbara Henning and Patricia Spears Jones" for The Poetry Project Newsletter , drafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence consists of letters and printed email exchanges with poets, writers, artists, editors, publishers, family, friends, and others. Correspondents include American poets Charles Alexander, Bill Kushner, Harryette Mullen, and Maureen Owen, and American writer Harry Mathews, as well as American authors Charles Baxter and E. M. Broner, who were Henning's professors at Wayne State University. The series also includes a small amount of writings and artworks shared by correspondents.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal Papers consists of clippings, college essays, and publicity materials such as publisher's catalogues advertising Henning's books and fliers for readings.

did.unittitle: Poetry and fiction readings flier, University of Houston-Victoria

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Computer Media, and Audiovisual Media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 17 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 18 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains computer media including Henning's born digital writing files, emails, digital photos, and audio recordings on external hard drives and optical media. It also includes electronic media separated from Series II. Correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains audio recordings of readings and interviews.

did.unittitle: "Melinda Miel - The Law of the Dream"
